title: Promote the recovered shell family into the normal package path
summary: 0.5.0 starts the shell lane by turning the recovered laptop, phone, tablet, modern TV, and retro TV variants into first-class packaged assets with packaged presets, instead of leaving them as adjacent recovery artifacts.
reasoning: The shell family is already real in the repo, but it has not been part of the normal shipping-style package path. The smallest useful 0.5.0 step is to package those shells cleanly and give them stable self-contained preset identity so future shell validation and release work rides the same process as the main player.

changes.summary: Adds optional shell-family staging to the normal var packager so recovered phone, tablet, laptop, and TV shell assets can ship beside the main player asset in the same package.
changes.reasoning: This promotes the shell family into the real product path instead of keeping it as a separate recovery lane.

changes.summary: Generates shell presets with var-scoped asset URLs and the packaged player plugin attached, rather than copying raw Custom-path presets into the package.
changes.reasoning: A packaged shell should behave like a self-contained product asset, not a leftover dev preset.

changes.summary: Moves shell export prefabs off the temporary Unity asset path so bundled shell asset names are cleaner and more stable.
changes.reasoning: This reduces temp-path leakage into bundled asset identity and aligns the shell lane with the leaner packaging direction.

verification: Re-export the CUA shell family from the current ghost training Unity project and confirm the summary points at the current repo lane with non-temp bundled asset names.

Build the player package with shell-family inclusion enabled and verify the var contains shell assetbundles and packaged shell presets under Custom/Assets/FrameAngel/Player and Custom/Atom/CustomUnityAsset.
Confirm the packaged shell presets reference var-scoped asset URLs and the packaged player plugin instead of raw Custom paths.
knownIssues: This slice packages the recovered shell family but does not add new shell-specific runtime behaviors like phone or tablet swipe handling.
The composed host package path still does not carry a real screen aperture node because the current recovered shell exports leave that field empty.
